metadata.title: Build a Failure Criterion Generator with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Namaste, Liam — in for Bear. How does build a failure criterion generator with claude? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Build a Failure Criterion Generator with Claude?
props.segment: Build a Failure Criterion Generator with

beats.label: Hook — the missing question
beats.narration: 'What would make this wrong?' is the most under-used question in prompt engineering. It surfaces constraints you didn't know you had — until you try to articulate what bad looks like.

shots.notes: Large centered question mark. Below: 'What would make this wrong?' in CRIMSON. Smaller text: 'You can't know if output is good without knowing what bad looks like.' Teardown palette, cream background.

beats.label: Claude command
beats.narration: The seed command pipes any prompt into Claude and asks it to generate a failure criterion checklist — specific, testable, binary conditions that would make any output wrong.

props.command: claude "Generate a failure criterion checklist for this prompt: what specific, testable conditions would make any output wrong, incomplete, or misleading? Format as numbered checklist, binary pass/fail, 8-12 criteria." < prompt.txt

beats.label: Python code
beats.narration: The script generates the checklist, then applies each criterion to a sample output from the same prompt — lighting each one green or red as it checks.

props.code:
import subprocess, sys
prompt = open(sys.argv[1]).read()
output = open(sys.argv[2]).read()
criteria_cmd = (
  "Generate failure criterion checklist for this "
  "prompt: testable, binary, pass/fail, 8-12 items."
)
criteria = subprocess.run(
  ["claude", criteria_cmd], input=prompt,
  capture_output=True, text=True
).stdout
print("CRITERIA:\n", criteria)

beats.label: Manim — failure criteria checklist
beats.narration: Six failure criteria animate in. Five flag red — genuine failure modes. One passes green. Each criterion is binary, specific, and requires no judgment call to apply.

beats.label: The change — add as constraints
beats.narration: Now take the three most common failure criteria and add them as negative constraints directly to the prompt. The rewritten prompt prevents the failures without needing a separate check.

props.command: claude "Rewrite this prompt adding these three failure criteria as explicit negative constraints: [cite source / bound to sample / define jargon]." < prompt.txt

beats.label: Changed output
beats.narration: The rewritten prompt outputs pass the three criteria automatically. No separate check required — the constraint closes the gap at the source.

shots.notes: Three rows: Criterion 1 (cite source) — BEFORE: FAIL / AFTER: PASS green. Criterion 2 (bound to sample) — BEFORE: FAIL / AFTER: PASS green. Criterion 3 (no jargon) — BEFORE: FAIL / AFTER: PASS green. Teardown palette.

beats.label: Teardown angle
beats.narration: The failure criterion checklist is the prompt's test suite. You can't know if an output is good without knowing what bad looks like — and what bad looks like is almost always more specific than you think.

shots.notes: Large text: 'The checklist is the prompt's test suite.' Below: two rows — 'Without it: review by feel.' / 'With it: review against criteria.' CRIMSON rule. Teardown palette.

beats.label: Next reel teaser
beats.narration: Next: the 3-pass refinement loop — replace random iteration with a structured diagnostic.

shots.notes: Clean: 'NEXT: 3-Pass Refinement Demo →' Minimal teardown style.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Build a Failure Criterion Generator with Claude] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
